payload.title: Corporate Governance Committee Meeting
payload.description: Corporate Governance Committee meeting materials including agenda, March 2024 meeting minutes, 2025 work plan, MTA and subsidiary by-laws, committee charters, and governance guidelines.

warnings: source.publisher is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Publisher has 39 accepted/canonical observations and should link to entity context when useful. Suggested relation: published_by.

payload.description: Board committee responsible for corporate governance policies, ethics, procurement guidelines, and whistleblower protections. Meetings held on March 25, 2024 and December 16, 2024.

warnings: entity.parent_entity is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Parent entities are relation context. Suggested relation: part_of_agency.
entity.agency is relation context, not a closed enum or identity key. Agency names are relation context.

payload.description: Corporate Governance Committee meeting held at 2 Broadway, 20th Floor, Board Room 2, from 11:45 AM - 12:15 PM ET. Agenda included public comments, approval of March 2024 minutes, 2025 work plan, review of by-laws, committee charters, governance guidelines, and MTA policies.

Meeting called to order at 12:25 p.m. at 2 Broadway – 20th Floor, New York, NY. Agenda included procurement guidelines, code of ethics, mission statement and performance indicators report, whistleblower policy, and policies requiring board approval. Adjourned at 12:38pm.

tool_args.label: MTA Mission Statement and Performance Indicator Report

payload.claim_text: The Committee approved the MTA Mission Statement and Performance Indicator Report to the Authority Budget Office.
payload.subject: MTA Corporate Governance Committee
payload.description: Committee approved the 2023 MTA Mission Statement, Measures and Performance Indicators Report at the March 25, 2024 meeting, as required by the Public Authorities Law.

payload.claim_text: Section 2879 of the Public Authorities Law requires the Board to annually review the Procurement Guidelines. There are no recommended changes at this time.
payload.subject: MTA Procurement Guidelines
payload.description: Committee reviewed procurement guidelines and recommended Board approval with no changes at the March 25, 2024 meeting.

payload.claim_text: The MTA maintains a public data dashboard at metrics.mta.info for each of the MTA operating agencies which are updated monthly, summarizing the agencies' leading performance measurements. This data is also routinely posted to the New York State Open Data Portal.
payload.subject: MTA performance data
payload.description: MTA Board and committees monitor agency operating performance through monthly reports, committee books, and public dashboard at metrics.mta.info.

tool_args.label: MTA Agencies List
tool_args.raw_text: The MTA Agencies consist of: Metropolitan Transportation Authority Triborough Bridge and Tunnel Authority New York City Transit Authority Manhattan and Bronx Surface Transit Operating Authority The Long Island Rail Road Company Metro-North Commuter Railroad Company Staten Island Rapid Transit Operating Authority Metropolitan Suburban Bus Authority MTA Bus Company MTA Construction and Development Company First Mutual Transportation Assurance Company MTA Grand Central Madison Operating Company

payload.claim_text: The MTA Agencies consist of: Metropolitan Transportation Authority, Triborough Bridge and Tunnel Authority, New York City Transit Authority, Manhattan and Bronx Surface Transit Operating Authority, The Long Island Rail Road Company, Metro-North Commuter Railroad Company, Staten Island Rapid Transit Operating Authority, Metropolitan Suburban Bus Authority, MTA Bus Company, MTA Construction and Development Company, First Mutual Transportation Assurance Company, MTA Grand Central Madison Operating Company.
payload.subject: MTA Agencies
payload.description: List of agencies under common control of MTA as defined in the Governance Guidelines.

tool_args.label: Board Committees Established
tool_args.raw_text: The Chair has established the following Committees to assist them and the Board in discharging their responsibilities: (1) the Audit Committee; (2) the Committee on Finance; (3) the Committee on Operations of the New York City Transit Authority, the Manhattan and Bronx Surface Transit Operating Authority, the Staten Island Rapid Transit Operating Authority and the MTA Bus Company; (4) the Committee on Operation of the Metro-North Commuter Railroad; (5) the Committee on Operation of the Long Island Rail Road; (6) the Committee on Operations of the Triborough Bridge and Tunnel Authority; (7) the Capital Program Committee; (8) the Diversity Committee; (9) the Corporate Governance Committee; and (10) the Safety Committee.

payload.claim_text: The Chair has established the following Committees: (1) the Audit Committee; (2) the Committee on Finance; (3) the Committee on Operations of the New York City Transit Authority, the Manhattan and Bronx Surface Transit Operating Authority, the Staten Island Rapid Transit Operating Authority and the MTA Bus Company; (4) the Committee on Operation of the Metro-North Commuter Railroad; (5) the Committee on Operation of the Long Island Rail Road; (6) the Committee on Operations of the Triborough Bridge and Tunnel Authority; (7) the Capital Program Committee; (8) the Diversity Committee; (9) the Corporate Governance Committee; and (10) the Safety Committee.
payload.subject: MTA Board committees

payload.claim_text: The Governance Guidelines, amended December 20, 2023, provide the framework for the governance of the MTA and its subsidiaries and component units, along with the laws of the State of New York, the MTA By-laws, the by-laws of certain MTA subsidiaries and component units, and the charters and key practices of certain Committees of the Board.
payload.subject: MTA Governance Guidelines
payload.description: The Governance Guidelines were amended December 20, 2023, and cover functions of the MTA Chair/CEO, Board, senior management, committees, self-assessment, meeting agendas, ethics/conflicts of interest, and oversight of significant MTA policies.

payload.claim_text: The December 2024 Corporate Governance Committee agenda included review of by-laws for: Metropolitan Transportation Authority (approved March 21, 2018), MTA Construction and Development Company (approved December 18, 2019), First Mutual Transportation Assurance Company (approved July 24, 2013), MTA Grand Central Madison Operating Company (approved October 20, 2021), Long Island Rail Road (approved July 24, 2013), Metro-North Railroad (approved July 24, 2013), MTA Bus Company (approved July 24, 2013), New York City Transit Authority (approved July 24, 2013), Staten Island Rapid Transit Authority (approved July 24, 2013), and Triborough Bridges and Tunnels Authority (approved July 24, 2013).
payload.subject: MTA By-laws review

payload.claim_text: The Metropolitan Transportation Authority preserves and enhances the quality of life and economic health of the region we serve through cost-efficient provision of safe, on-time, reliable and clean transportation services.
payload.subject: MTA Mission Statement
payload.description: Official MTA mission statement as stated in the Governance Guidelines.

tool_args.label: Board Committee Charters
tool_args.raw_text: 5. REVIEW BOARD COMMITTEE CHARTERS Audit Committee Charter - Page 78 Corporate Governance Committee Charter - Page 85 CPC Committee Charter - Page 88 Diversity Committee Charter - Page 92 Finance Committee Charter - Page 95 Safety Committee Charter - Page 98 LIRR Committee Charter - Page 101 MNR Committee Charter - Page 104 NYCTA MTA Bus Committee Charter - Page 107 TBTA Committee Charter - Page 110

payload.claim_text: The December 2024 Corporate Governance Committee meeting agenda included review of the following committee charters: Audit Committee, Corporate Governance Committee, Capital Program Committee (CPC), Diversity Committee, Finance Committee, Safety Committee, LIRR Committee, MNR Committee, NYCTA/MTA Bus Committee, and TBTA Committee.
payload.subject: Board committee charters

payload.claim_text: The 2025 Corporate Governance Committee Work Plan includes recurring agenda items (approval of minutes, governance work plan, review of significant governance issues, follow-up items, executive sessions) and specific agenda items for February 2025 (presentation on developments in corporate governance, review mission statement/measurement report, procurement guidelines, whistleblower policy, code of ethics, MTA policies under Public Authorities Law) and October 2025 (procurement lobby law compliance, governance principles, any revisions to by-laws, committee charters, MTA policies, and approve 2026 work plan).
payload.subject: MTA Corporate Governance Committee Work Plan 2025

payload.claim_text: The Chair of the MTA shall be primarily responsible for providing leadership to the MTA Board in performing oversight of the senior management in the effective and ethical management of the MTA Agencies' integrated mass transportation system. The Chair, pursuant to statute, is the Chief Executive Officer of the Authority and shall have responsibility to discharge the executive and administrative functions and powers of the Authority.
payload.subject: MTA Chair/CEO
payload.description: Governance Guidelines section on the functions of the MTA Chair/Chief Executive Officer.

payload.claim_text: The Governance Guidelines cover: Functions of the MTA Board (meet no less than 11 times a year, general oversight of senior management, specific functions including financial strategies, risk assessment, integrity, policies); Senior Management (Chair/CEO serves ex officio as chair and CEO of each affiliated and subsidiary agency, Presidents of constituent agencies responsible for general management); Committees of the Board (10 committees listed); Self-Assessment (annual for Board and each Committee); Agendas (Chair responsible); Ethics and Conflicts of Interest; Oversight of Significant MTA Policies; Access to Independent Advisors; New MTA Board Member Orientation.
payload.subject: MTA Governance Guidelines
payload.description: Summary of the governance guidelines provisions pages 2-4

payload.claim_text: The principal office of the Metropolitan Transportation Authority shall be located in the City of New York, County of New York.
payload.subject: MTA By-laws

payload.claim_text: These by-laws may be altered, amended or repealed and new by-laws may be adopted by the board at any regular or special meeting as to which the nature of the proposed alterations, amendments or repeals have been sent in writing to the members of the board together with the notice of meeting if it is a special meeting or if at a regular meeting at least seventy-two hours in advance of such regular meeting.
payload.subject: MTA By-laws Amendments

payload.claim_text: In case of any conflict between or among governing documents and/or statutory provisions, the following order of precedence applies: Statutes; Articles of Incorporation; By-Laws; Committee Charters; and Governance Guidelines.
payload.subject: MTA governing documents
payload.description: Article VIII of MTA By-laws establishes order of precedence for governing documents.

payload.claim_text: The Audit Committee shall consist of at least 3 or more members of the Board, appointed by the Board Chair. At least one committee member shall have accounting or financial management expertise. No member of the Committee shall be employed by (a) the MTA, or (b) a private entity that does, or is likely to do, business with the MTA.
payload.subject: Audit Committee

payload.claim_text: The Audit Committee shall meet on a regularly-scheduled basis at least 4 times per year, and more frequently as circumstances dictate.
payload.subject: Audit Committee meetings

payload.claim_text: The Corporate Governance Committee shall assist the Board Chair and the Board in: (i) developing and recommending to the Board, policies to promote honest and ethical conduct by Board members, officers, and employees, and enhance public confidence in the MTA; (ii) developing, recommending to the Board and overseeing implementation of MTA policies relating to corporate governance, including the MTA Corporate Governance Principles; and (iii) reviewing on a regular basis the overall corporate governance of the MTA and recommending improvements when necessary.
payload.subject: Corporate Governance Committee
payload.description: Purpose of the Corporate Governance Committee as stated in its charter

payload.claim_text: The Capital Program Committee (CPC) shall assist the Board Chair and the Board in fulfilling their responsibility to monitor the effective and efficient implementation of the MTA's five-year capital program.
payload.subject: Capital Program Committee

payload.claim_text: The Diversity Committee shall assist the Board Chair and the Board by reviewing, providing guidance, and making recommendations with respect to the diversity programs and initiatives undertaken by MTA and its subsidiary and affiliate agencies.
payload.subject: Diversity Committee

payload.claim_text: The Finance Committee shall assist the Board Chair and the Board by (1) reviewing, providing guidance, and making recommendations with respect to the MTA's core financial policies and (2) reviewing, providing guidance and making recommendations with respect to MTA real estate matters.
payload.subject: Finance Committee

payload.claim_text: The Safety Committee shall assist the Board Chair and the Board by reviewing, providing guidance, and making recommendations with respect to the management of safety on an MTA-wide basis. The MTA manages safety through its Safety Management System (SMS) which is a top-down, organization wide, data driven approach to managing safety risk.
payload.subject: Safety Committee

payload.claim_text: The Committee on Operations of the Long Island Rail Road and the Metropolitan Suburban Bus Authority (LIRR/LIB) shall assist the Board Chair and the Board in fulfilling their responsibility to monitor and oversee the operations of the Long Island Rail Road Company and the Metropolitan Suburban Bus Authority.
payload.subject: LIRR/LIB Committee

payload.claim_text: The Committee on Operations of the New York City Transit Authority, the Manhattan and Bronx Surface Transit Operating Authority, the Staten Island Rapid Transit Operating Authority and the MTA Bus Company shall assist the Board Chair and the Board in fulfilling their responsibility to monitor and oversee the operations of NYCT and MTA Bus.
payload.subject: NYCT/MTA Bus Committee

tool_args.label: Corporate Governance Committee part of MTA

payload.description: MTA Corporate Governance Committee is a board committee of the Metropolitan Transportation Authority.
